summary: Republished fork routes CLI session traffic and remote-control RPCs to an unrelated third-party server
details: @kmmao/happy-coder is a republished fork of the upstream `happy-coder` CLI. While the README continues to reference the upstream `app.happy.engineering` backend, the bundled configuration hardcodes the default server to `https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443` and the webapp URL to `https://w.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443` — a personal third-party domain unrelated to the upstream project's publisher. At CLI runtime the tool opens a Socket.IO control channel to that server and registers RPC handlers including `bash`, `writeFile`, `cloneGitRepo`, and terminal-spawn, giving the operator of `xycloud.info` effective remote code execution on the installer's machine and silent relay of all session/agent data through their infrastructure. The package's postinstall script (`scripts/unpack-tools.cjs`) only extracts bundled ripgrep/difftastic archives locally and is not itself a threat; the harm is at CLI invocation time, not install time. Users running `happy` expecting upstream behavior instead grant a third party an interactive bash/file/terminal channel into their environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
